data.title: Electrolux Group Interim report Q1 2026
data.description: In the first quarter of 2026, in Stockholm, net sales totaled SEK 29,543 million, down from SEK 32,576 million in the same period. There was a slight decline in organic sales by -0.5%, compared to a positive growth of 7.9% previously. However, organic sales showed a promising increase of +3.6% in Europe, Middle East & Africa, as well as Asia Pacific. In Latin America, the growth rate was even higher at +8.0%.

data.title: CLINUVEL receives final EMA scientific advice for pivotal Phase III vitiligo study
data.description: A comprehensive assessment was conducted on SCENESSE® using a "totality of evidence" approach. This approach analyzed all available data to evaluate the drug. The thorough investigation considered various aspects, including efficacy, safety, and tolerability. SCENESSE® showed promising results across multiple parameters, making it a potentially beneficial treatment option. This meticulous evaluation process aimed to ensure a well-rounded understanding of the medication's overall performance. The approach's systematic review considered a wide range of factors, leading to a comprehensive assessment. Initial findings highlight the positive outcomes associated with SCENESSE®, indicating its potential as an effective therapy. This methodical analysis serves to provide a detailed overview of the drug's performance based on the accumulated evidence, presenting a holistic perspective on its effectiveness. Further exploration and validation are essential to solidify these initial results and confirm SCENESSE®'s impact on patient outcomes.

data.title: Volvo Group - the first quarter 2026
data.description: In Gothenburg, Sweden, on April 24, 2026, the Volvo Group reported strong financial performance for the first quarter of 2026. Despite a decrease in market volumes compared to the previous year, the company showcased resilience and operational excellence, leading to a solid profitability with an adjusted operating margin of 11.0%. The company's ability to maintain profitability in a challenging market environment underscores its strength and strategic management.

data.title: 7 crypto protocols commit 43,000 ETH for rsETH collateral restoration in "DeFi United" rescue initiative.
data.description: Several organizations, including Mantle, EtherFi Foundation, Golem Foundation, Lido DAO, Ethena, LayerZero, Ink Foundation, and Tyrdo, have collectively pledged support to the "DeFi United" recovery initiative. The combined commitment from these groups is aimed at aiding in the effort to rebuild and strengthen the decentralized finance ecosystem. This alliance reflects a significant show of solidarity within the DeFi community in an effort to address current challenges and work towards a more resilient future.
